data_window_note: MMS L2 data ~90 days behind real-time

unusual_events.explanation: 309 electron holes (bipolar E∥ events) detected in 6 hours. This is unusually high activity, indicating an active or recently saturated two-stream instability. These phase-space vortices are the direct nonlinear saturation products of Langmuir wave trapping (Bernstein et al. 1957, Graham et al. 2016 GRL).

Unusually steep magnetic power spectrum: α = -2.507 (much steeper than Kolmogorov α = -5/3 = -1.667). This indicates enhanced dissipation at sub-ion scales, possibly due to Landau damping of kinetic Alfvén waves or reconnection-driven turbulence. Active Langmuir/TSI waves can inject energy into the perpendicular direction, feeding into the turbulent cascade.

electron_holes.note: 309 bipolar E∥ events above 1.16 mV/m — consistent with nonlinear TSI saturation products (electron holes/phase-space vortices)

electrons.beam_note: No clear beam — mean Ve=230.3 km/s < 300 km/s threshold.
electrons.dfdv_note: v_beam/v_th = 0.01 < 1.5 → no bump-on-tail. Beam insufficient for classical TSI.

theory_validation.validation_note: Beam velocity too low for classical TSI at observed conditions.

tsi_weibel_chain.note: Chain not detected — no electron beam present

langmuir_proxy.note: f_pe=13.39 kHz outside EDP frequency range (Nyquist ~16 Hz for slow survey)
